articles.title: An open-source bot that only bets "no" on Polymarket is making money
articles.summary: A new open-source bot is profiting by systematically betting against propositions on Polymarket, suggesting the platform's markets consistently overprice the probability of events happening. This reveals a potentially exploitable bias where users get too excited about dramatic outcomes, creating easy money for contrarians willing to bet on the boring reality that most hyped events don't materialize.

articles.title: Polymarket Prediction Market Puts 54% Odds on Hormuz Blockade Ending by April 30
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are betting 54% odds that any blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will be resolved by April 30, suggesting markets expect relatively quick diplomatic or military resolution to one of the world's most critical shipping chokepoints. This represents a fascinating test case for prediction markets pricing geopolitical risk, especially since a prolonged Hormuz closure would crater global oil supplies and trigger massive economic disruption.

articles.title: Kalshi ramps up commodities expansion amid dynamic geopolitical environment
articles.summary: Kalshi is expanding its commodities trading offerings as global political tensions create more volatility in energy, agriculture, and raw materials markets. Smart money recognizes that geopolitical chaos makes commodity prices harder to predict through traditional analysis, creating profitable opportunities for skilled prediction market traders who can read political tea leaves.

articles.title: Pennsylvania county bans poll workers from using election prediction markets
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania county has prohibited poll workers from betting on election outcomes while on duty. The move signals growing concern that prediction markets could influence election administration, potentially leading to broader restrictions as betting on politics gains mainstream attention.

articles.title: Kalshi Expands Commodity Predictions Market Amid War Volatility
articles.summary: Kalshi is launching new commodity prediction markets as geopolitical tensions drive wild price swings in oil, wheat, and other raw materials. War creates the perfect storm for commodity betting , massive volatility, information asymmetries, and traders desperate for hedging tools beyond traditional futures markets.

articles.title: Magic vs 76ers Win Probability at Prediction Markets Like Kalshi
articles.summary: Prediction markets like Kalshi are offering live win probability odds on the Magic vs 76ers matchup, giving bettors real-time data on how the game's momentum shifts throughout play. This represents the growing sophistication of sports betting markets, where traditional point spreads are being supplemented by dynamic probability models that update constantly based on game flow and situational factors.

articles.title: Prediction markets are breaking the news and becoming their own beat
articles.summary: Prediction markets are increasingly becoming the first place major news breaks, with traders moving odds before traditional media even catches wind of developing stories. This shift turns prediction platforms into must-watch news sources themselves, giving sharp bettors who monitor market movements a serious information edge over everyone else.

articles.title: Kalshi Survivor 50 Episode 8 Predictions - Who Goes Home on Survivor Tonight?
articles.summary: Kalshi is running prediction markets on who gets voted off Survivor tonight in Episode 8 of Season 50. Reality TV betting markets are exploding as platforms discover that devoted fan bases will trade on literally anything they can obsess over.

articles.title: Prediction markets are a gamble we can't afford | Editorial
articles.summary: Some editorial board thinks prediction markets are too risky and we should avoid them entirely. Classic regulatory fear-mongering that could fuel more government crackdowns on platforms where people actually put money behind their predictions.

articles.title: Ohio slaps Kalshi with $5 million fine for unlicensed sports betting
articles.summary: Ohio regulators hit Kalshi with a $5 million penalty for allegedly operating unlicensed sports betting markets in the state. The fine signals growing regulatory scrutiny of prediction market platforms that blur the line between political forecasting and traditional gambling, potentially forcing clearer boundaries across the industry.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Gambling?
articles.summary: Regulators and critics keep labeling prediction markets as gambling, threatening their legal status and mainstream adoption. The distinction matters because gambling laws could shut down platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi, while "information markets" get to operate freely and attract institutional money.

articles.title: Prediction Market Risk Is Hiding in Your Organization Whether You Know It or Not
articles.summary: Every company makes dozens of implicit predictions daily about customer behavior, market conditions, and operational outcomes, but most executives don't recognize these forecasts as bets that could blow up their strategy. Smart organizations are starting to formalize these hidden predictions into trackable markets, giving them better odds of spotting disasters before they hit the bottom line.
